content.problemStatement:
You are given a directed weighted graph with PPP vertices, labelled from 000 to P‚àí1P-1P‚àí1. PPP is a prime number. You are also given an integer KKK.

For each vertex uuu, there is an edge from vertex uuu to vertex (K‚ãÖu+i)‚Äämod‚ÄäP(K \cdot u + i) \bmod P(K‚ãÖu+i)modP for all 0‚â§i‚â§K‚àí10 \leq i \leq K-10‚â§i‚â§K‚àí1. The weight of an edge directed from the vertex uuu to the vertex (K‚ãÖu+i)‚Äämod‚ÄäP(K \cdot u + i) \bmod P(K‚ãÖu+i)modP is equal to iii.

You will be given QQQ queries. In each query you will be given two vertices XXX and YYY and you have to determine the minimum cost of going from XXX to YYY. If a path does not exists between XXX and YYY, print ‚àí1-1‚àí1.


Input Format

Each test contains multiple test cases. The first line contains the number of test cases TTT. The description of TTT testcases as follows.
The first line of each test contains 333 space separated integers P,K,QP, K, QP,K,Q.
Then QQQ lines follow. Each of the QQQ lines contains two space separated integers XXX and YYY denoting a query.


Output Format
For each query print a single integer denoting the minimum cost of going from XXX to YYY. If a path does not exist print ‚àí1-1‚àí1.
constraints: 2‚â§P‚â§1062 \leq P \leq 10^62‚â§P‚â§106 and PPP is a prime
